title: In-Solution Trypsin digestion of Proteins for MS analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ems19","text":"This protocol describes a standardized workflow for in-solucion trypsin digestion of protein samples intended for proteomic identification via mass spectrometry (MS). This procedure ensures the production of high-quality peptides while minimizing contamination and ensuring reproducibility and efficiency.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"36fta","text":"Always handle samples under clean, keratin‑controlled conditions. Wear powder‑free gloves (preferably nitrile), a lab coat and a hair cover at all times to minimize the risk of keratin contamination.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"au8b6","text":"Ideally, the sample should have a protein concentration of 1 mg/mL.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"62gtn","text":"Precipitate samples to remove contaminants incompatible with trypsin digestion, including protease inhibitors, detergents/surfactants (e.g., SDS, Triton X‑100), chaotropes (urea, guanidine), chelators (EDTA/EGTA), organic solvents, salts/buffers (e.g., Tris, phosphate, HEPES), glycerol and unquenched alkylating agents.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d9jds","text":"Use LC-MS grade reagents.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9i43n","text":"Use low-protein binding microtubes (avoid autoclaved or PCR-coated microtubes) and low-binding pipette tips.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4ag4h","text":"Incubate Iodoacetamide (IAA) step in the dark to avoid side reactions (step 3).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dhh5s","text":"Handle Formic acid (FA) with the appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) and follow the corresponding waste‑management procedures.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2is40","text":"Store Trypsin stock aliquots at -20 °C for up to 6 months and avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Add 250 mM dithiothreitol (DTT) to the sample to reach a final concentration of 10 mM and incubate for 40 min at 56 °C, with agitation (650 rpm), to reduce disulfide bridges. Allow the sample to cool afterwards.
Add 500 mM iodoacetamide (IAA) to the sample to reach a final concentration of 20 mM and incubate for 30 min at room temperature, protected from light, to alkylate the reduced disulfide bridges.
Add 250 mM DTT to the sample to raise the final DTT concentration to 21 mM and incubate for 10 min at room temperature, protected from light, to quench any remaining IAA.
Precipitate the sample by adding six volumes of ice-cold acetone, vortex briefly to mix and incubate overnight at −20°C.
Centrifuge at 16,000 × g for 10 minutes using a pre-cooled rotor. Carefully remove the supernatant and allow the pellet to air-dry for 5 min.
Resuspend the pellet in 50 mM ammonium bicarbonate (NH₄HCO₃) by vortexing for 5 min, then sonicate for 10 min to ensure complete protein solubilization.

Add trypsin to the sample at a 1:50 (enzyme:protein, w/w) ratio and incubate overnight at 37°C with agitation (650 rpm). Alternative Proteases and Conditions: Trypsin+Lys-C or Chymotrypsin Concentration: 10 ng/µLDigestion buffer: 50 mM NH₄HCO₃Temperature: 37ºC Asp-N Concentration: 20 ng/µLDigestion buffer: 50 mM NH₄HCO₃Temperature: 37ºC Glu-C Concentration: 25 ng/µLDigestion buffer: 25 mM NH₄HCO₃Temperature: 25ºC
Spin down and stop the digestion by acidifying with Formic acid (FA) to a final concentration of 5%.
Aspirate 100 µL of 50% ACN to clean/activate the C18 tip. Discard the solution and repeat once more.
Mass Spectrometry Unit (UniMS) E-mail: unims.technicians@itqb.unl.ptSite: http://www.itqb.unl.pt/facilities/UniMS/
Aspirate 100 µL of 5% FA to equilibrate the C18 tip. Discard the liquid and repeat once more.
Aspirate and dispense the sample five times to ensure maximum peptide binding to the C18 tip.
Aspirate 100 µL of 5% FA to clean the peptides. Discard the supernatant and repeat once more.
Aspirate 100 µL of 90% ACN + 0.1% FA and elute the purified peptides into a new low-protein binding microtube.
Dry the purified peptides completely using a SpeedVac.
Store the purified samples and the flow‑throughs at −20 °C.

Quantify the sample before beginning the protocol. Ideally, the sample should have a protein concentration of 1 mg/mL. Note: To minimize keratin contamination, perform all steps of this protocol under clean, keratin‑controlled conditions. Wear powder‑free gloves (preferably nitrile), a lab coat and a hair cover at all times.
Add 1 M NH₄HCO₃ to non‑precipitated samples to reach a final concentration of 50 mM NH₄HCO₃.
Reconstitute the contents of one 20 µg vial of trypsin (e.g., Promega V5111) in 200 µL of 1 mM HCl. Aliquot the reconstituted trypsin into 10 µL portions and store at −20°C for up to 6 months.
creator: unims.technicians
